replies.author: @BootWizard
replies.text:
The Kaizen launching relative to the previous BioTouch I don't think is an issue, same as any brand launching 2 boots together or in quick succession, and I wouldn't consider the initial soleplate change a new variation either, it's a running change which we see a lot across all manufacturers. 

So you've got the Scudetta & the Kaizen in FG & AG. No issues there. 

Then you've got the BioTouch, my issue being that there has been almost a full redesign between the launch colourway and the second colourway. Outside of that it's not an excessive number of variations of a boot.

With relation to the bend points, as I mentioned in the video there is 2 bend points, but I feel that the one towards the toes needs to be adjusted or made to be more effective. Also the outsole toe flexibility has never been a main issue with any Scudetta, the main issue for the majority of people was the low pointed toebox which the BioTouch Sapphire does address. 

Also for the sake of clarity for anyone reading, I also didn't say "all issues addressed" in this video, despite you putting that in quotation marks as if I did.

replies.text: ​@BootWizarddepends on the person, for me the sole bend or lack of gradualness bothered more than the toebox. I do prefer the fg in all ways though, just fits and feels nicer. Good point, I out the new Biotouch and Kaizen in the same category in my head as I assume they have the same redesigns

comments.author: @spacemanspiff19
comments.text:
Just my luck that I bought the Scudetta AG in the Brazil colorway a couple weeks ago, and now this is about to release 😆I did get to take advantage of that big sale though. Plus I absolutely love the yellow colorway, so I can't kick myself too much. That little bit of extra volume in the toebox on these would've been nice though. 

I'm a little disappointed they went with a thinner insole on these. Some brands give you a thin, crappy insole that does nothing for underfoot comfort even on their elite models. I like that Sokito's insole was a bit nicer than those. Wish they would have just increased the toebox volume a little bit and kept the original insole thickness. Not the end of the world I suppose.

I think I could maybe climb that hill with you about the forefoot stiffness of the outsole. It's a mixed bag for me. On one hand, I find it very fun to run in because of how responsive and snappy it is. On the other hand, a bit more flexibility toward the front of the outsole might be better for underfoot comfort, especially on AG pitches. 

At any rate, overall I love what Sokito is doing. Excited to see what the future holds for them. A TF model at some point hopefully?

replies.author: @BootWizard
replies.text:
The Kaizen launching relative to the previous BioTouch I don't think is an issue, same as any brand launching 2 boots together or in quick succession, and I wouldn't consider the initial soleplate change a new variation either, it's a running change which we see a lot across all manufacturers. 

So you've got the Scudetta & the Kaizen in FG & AG. No issues there. 

Then you've got the BioTouch, my issue being that there has been almost a full redesign between the launch colourway and the second colourway. Outside of that it's not an excessive number of variations of a boot.

With relation to the bend points, as I mentioned in the video there is 2 bend points, but I feel that the one towards the toes needs to be adjusted or made to be more effective. Also the outsole toe flexibility has never been a main issue with any Scudetta, the main issue for the majority of people was the low pointed toebox which the BioTouch Sapphire does address. 

Also for the sake of clarity for anyone reading, I also didn't say "all issues addressed" in this video, despite you putting that in quotation marks as if I did.

comments.author: @spacemanspiff19
comments.text:
Just my luck that I bought the Scudetta AG in the Brazil colorway a couple weeks ago, and now this is about to release 😆I did get to take advantage of that big sale though. Plus I absolutely love the yellow colorway, so I can't kick myself too much. That little bit of extra volume in the toebox on these would've been nice though. 

I'm a little disappointed they went with a thinner insole on these. Some brands give you a thin, crappy insole that does nothing for underfoot comfort even on their elite models. I like that Sokito's insole was a bit nicer than those. Wish they would have just increased the toebox volume a little bit and kept the original insole thickness. Not the end of the world I suppose.

I think I could maybe climb that hill with you about the forefoot stiffness of the outsole. It's a mixed bag for me. On one hand, I find it very fun to run in because of how responsive and snappy it is. On the other hand, a bit more flexibility toward the front of the outsole might be better for underfoot comfort, especially on AG pitches. 

At any rate, overall I love what Sokito is doing. Excited to see what the future holds for them. A TF model at some point hopefully?

comments.author: @Fr1nc0marc0
comments.text:
LOS MEJORES Y PEORES PACKS DEL MUNDIAL (no cuentan signatures)
🏆 BREAKOUT PACK repite mísmo patrón de la Eurocopa y Copa América 2024, (MAD BRILLANCE PACK) ese rosa intenso encantó a muchos y fue la más llamativa a diferencia de @adidas que apostó por el ADVANCEMENT PACK. con tonos menos atrevidos.
🥈 ROAT TO GLORY PACK repetimos mismo patrón en el pack del mundial de clubes, con la diferencia que en esta edición también se viste de colores atrevidas, pero no me terminan de convencer parace un pack sin brillo y monocolor, copa, F50 y predator que sólo por la versión FT se salva con un negro en la lengüeta. Es un pack olvidable, no cómo las del mundial 2022 AL RIHALA PACK dónde hasta ahora sigue en mi cabeza por los colores, que dan ese brillo al mundial y Adidas se me quedó en medio. sólo lo salvó por los detalles en la caja, pegatinas y la copa del mundo que lleva en la talonera.
🥉 PRISM WHITE PACK de Mizuno, hace poco salió aunque pocos jugadores las llevarán puestas lo pongo en éste top por qué tiene esa filosofía diferente al resto, ser una bota elegante y sobria sin resalte de esos colores que gritan atención. pero este pack rompe ese esquema, los colores brillantes en la talonera en base de tono blanco le queda exquisito en su silo Neo, Morelia y Alpha.
🤢🤮🤮🤮 @PUMA SHOWTIME PACK vamos a a ver le copias a Nike un pack que ya salió antes, segundo te aferras a lo que funcionó en 2014 TRICKS, pumita éso ya pasó hacé más de 10 años, lo que me caga es que lo repites una y otra vez ..en la Eurocopa repetiste el patrón con tonos naranjas, Antes 2023 sacaate un REMAKE, después el año pasado repites el patrón con las RE-CHARGE PACK, es que no tienen otras ideas.... éste 2026 repites el mismo patrón déjate de j0deR Pumita.... estamos en un mundial, no se presenta una nueva generación innovadora, aplasta o se competitivo con las otras marcas... estamos en un mundial.... encima la generación 9 es la misma tomadura de pelo, retrocedemos a la generación 7 sólo que con otro nombre ya que la generación 8 fue un fracaso... ultra va sin rumbo y king 20 se salva por esta nueva generación que en verdad me encantó, cambiando materiales ETC etcétera.....
